name: installer

metadata.name: installer
metadata.summary: A library for installing Python wheels.

documentation:
# installer

<!-- start readme-pitch -->

This is a low-level library for installing a Python package from a
[wheel distribution](https://packaging.python.org/glossary/#term-Wheel). It
provides basic functionality and abstractions for handling wheels and installing
packages from wheels.

- Logic for "unpacking" a wheel (i.e. installation).
- Abstractions for various parts of the unpacking process.
- Extensible simple implementations of the abstractions.
- Platform-independent Python script wrapper generation.

<!-- end readme-pitch -->

You can read more in the [documentation](https://installer.rtfd.io/).

modules.name: installer.scripts
documentation: Generate executable scripts, on various platforms.

functions.name: installer.scripts._build_shebang
documentation:
Build a shebang line.

The non-launcher cases are taken directly from distlib's implementation,
which tries its best to account for command length, spaces in path, etc.

https://bitbucket.org/pypa/distlib/src/58cd5c6/distlib/scripts.py#lines-124

classes.name: installer.scripts.InvalidScript
documentation: Raised if the user provides incorrect script section or kind.

classes.name: installer.scripts.Script

methods.name: installer.scripts.Script.__init__
documentation:
Construct a Script object.

```
param name

name of the script

param module

module path, to load the entry point from

param attr

final attribute access, for the entry point

param section

Denotes the "entry point section" where this was specified.
Valid values are "gui" and "console".

type section

str
```

methods.name: installer.scripts.Script.generate
documentation:
Generate a launcher for this script.

```
param executable

Path to the executable to invoke.

param kind

Which launcher template should be used.
Valid values are "posix", "win-ia32", "win-amd64" and
"win-arm".

type kind

str

raises InvalidScript

if no appropriate template is available.

return

The name and contents of the launcher file.
```
Describes a script based on an entry point declaration.

modules.name: installer._core
documentation: Core wheel installation logic.

functions.name: installer._core._process_WHEEL_file
documentation:
Process the WHEEL file, from `source`.

Returns the scheme that the archive root should go in.

functions.name: installer._core._determine_scheme
documentation: Determine which scheme to place given path in, from source.

functions.name: installer._core.install
documentation:
Install wheel described by `source` into `destination`.

```
param source

wheel to install.

param destination

where to write the wheel.

param additional_metadata

additional metadata files to generate, usually
generated by the caller.
```

modules.name: installer.utils
documentation: Utilities related to handling / interacting with wheel files.

functions.name: installer.utils.parse_metadata_file
documentation:
Parse {pep}`376` `PKG-INFO`-style metadata files.

`METADATA` and `WHEEL` files (as per {pep}`427`) use the same syntax
and can also be parsed using this function.

```
param contents

The entire contents of the file
```

functions.name: installer.utils.canonicalize_name
documentation:
Canonicalize a project name according to PEP-503.

:param name: The project name to canonicalize

functions.name: installer.utils.parse_wheel_filename
documentation:
Parse a wheel filename, into it's various components.

:param filename: The filename to parse

functions.name: installer.utils.copyfileobj_with_hashing
documentation:
Copy a buffer while computing the content's hash and size.

Copies the source buffer into the destination buffer while computing the
hash of the contents. Adapted from {any}`shutil.copyfileobj`.

```
param source

buffer holding the source data

param dest

destination buffer

param hash_algorithm

hashing algorithm

return

size, hash digest of the contents
```

functions.name: installer.utils.get_launcher_kind
documentation: Get the launcher kind for the current machine.

functions.name: installer.utils.fix_shebang
documentation:
Replace `#!python` shebang in a stream with the correct interpreter.

```
param stream

stream to modify

param interpreter

"correct interpreter" to substitute the shebang with

returns

A context manager, that provides an appropriately modified stream.
```

functions.name: installer.utils.construct_record_file
documentation:
Construct a RECORD file.

```
param records

records as passed into 

param prefix_for_scheme

function to get a prefix to add for RECORD entries, within a scheme

return

A stream that can be written to file. Must be closed by the caller.
```

functions.name: installer.utils.parse_entrypoints
documentation:
Parse `entry_points.txt`-style files.

```
param text

entire contents of the file

return

name of the script, module to use, attribute to call, kind of script (cli / gui)
```

functions.name: installer.utils._current_umask
documentation: Get the current umask which involves having to set it temporarily.

functions.name: installer.utils.make_file_executable
documentation: Make the file at the provided path executable.
Borrowed from:
https://github.com/pypa/pip/blob/0f21fb92/src/pip/_internal/utils/unpacking.py#L93

modules.name: installer.sources
documentation: Source of information about a wheel file.

classes.name: installer.sources.WheelSource

methods.name: installer.sources.WheelSource.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a WheelSource object.

```
param distribution

distribution name (like urllib3)

param version

version associated with the wheel
```

methods.name: installer.sources.WheelSource.dist_info_dir
documentation: Name of the dist-info directory.

methods.name: installer.sources.WheelSource.data_dir
documentation: Name of the data directory.

methods.name: installer.sources.WheelSource.dist_info_filenames
documentation:
Get names of all files in the dist-info directory.

Sample usage/behaviour:

```
>>> wheel_source.dist_info_filenames
['METADATA', 'WHEEL']
```

methods.name: installer.sources.WheelSource.read_dist_info
documentation:
Get contents, from `filename` in the dist-info directory.

Sample usage/behaviour:

```
>>> wheel_source.read_dist_info("METADATA")
...
```

```
param filename

name of the file
```

methods.name: installer.sources.WheelSource.validate_record
documentation:
Validate `RECORD` of the wheel.

This method should be called before {py:func}`install <installer.install>`
if validation is required.

methods.name: installer.sources.WheelSource.get_contents
documentation:
Sequential access to all contents of the wheel (including dist-info files).

This method should return an iterable. Each value from the iterable must be a
tuple containing 3 elements:

- record: 3-value tuple, to pass to
  {py:meth}`RecordEntry.from_elements <installer.records.RecordEntry.from_elements>`.
- stream: An {py:class}`io.BufferedReader` object, providing the contents of the
  file at the location provided by the first element (path).
- is_executable: A boolean, representing whether the item has an executable bit.

All paths must be relative to the root of the wheel.

Sample usage/behaviour:

```
>>> iterable = wheel_source.get_contents()
>>> next(iterable)
(('pkg/__init__.py', '', '0'), <...>, False)
```

This method may be called multiple times. Each iterable returned must
provide the same content upon reading from a specific file's stream.

Represents an installable wheel.

This is an abstract class, whose methods have to be implemented by subclasses.

classes.name: installer.sources._WheelFileValidationError
documentation: Raised when a wheel file fails validation.

classes.name: installer.sources._WheelFileBadDistInfo
documentation: Raised when a wheel file has issues around `.dist-info`.

classes.name: installer.sources.WheelFile

methods.name: installer.sources.WheelFile.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a WheelFile object.

:param f: An open zipfile, which will stay open as long as this object is used.

methods.name: installer.sources.WheelFile.open
documentation: Create a wheelfile from a given path.

methods.name: installer.sources.WheelFile.dist_info_dir
documentation: Name of the dist-info directory.

methods.name: installer.sources.WheelFile.dist_info_filenames
documentation: Get names of all files in the dist-info directory.

methods.name: installer.sources.WheelFile.read_dist_info
documentation: Get contents, from `filename` in the dist-info directory.

methods.name: installer.sources.WheelFile.validate_record
documentation:
Validate `RECORD` of the wheel.

This method should be called before {py:func}`install <installer.install>`
if validation is required.

File names will always be validated against `RECORD`.

If `validate_contents` is true, sizes and hashes of files
will also be validated against `RECORD`.

```
param validate_contents

Whether to validate content integrity.
```

methods.name: installer.sources.WheelFile.get_contents
documentation:
Sequential access to all contents of the wheel (including dist-info files).

This implementation requires that every file that is a part of the wheel
archive has a corresponding entry in RECORD. If they are not, an
{any}`AssertionError` will be raised.

Implements `WheelSource`, for an existing file from the filesystem.

Example usage:

```
>>> with WheelFile.open("sampleproject-2.0.0-py3-none-any.whl") as source:
...     installer.install(source, destination)
```

modules.name: installer.records
documentation: Provides an object-oriented model for handling {pep}`376` RECORD files.

functions.name: installer.records.parse_record_file
documentation:
Parse a {pep}`376` RECORD.

Returns an iterable of 3-value tuples, that can be passed to
{any}`RecordEntry.from_elements`.

```
param rows

iterator providing lines of a RECORD (no trailing newlines).
```

classes.name: installer.records.InvalidRecordEntry
documentation: Raised when a RecordEntry is not valid, due to improper element values or count.

classes.name: installer.records.Hash

methods.name: installer.records.Hash.__init__
documentation:
Construct a `Hash` object.

Most consumers should use {py:meth}`Hash.parse` instead, since no
validation or parsing is performed by this constructor.

```
param name

name of the hash function

param value

hashed value
```

methods.name: installer.records.Hash.validate
documentation:
Validate that `data` matches this instance.

```
param data

Contents of the file.

return

Whether data matches the hashed value.
```

methods.name: installer.records.Hash.parse
documentation:
Build a Hash object, from a "name=value" string.

This accepts a string of the format for the second element in a record,
as described in {pep}`376`.

Typical usage:

```
Hash.parse("sha256=Y0sCextp4SQtQNU-MSs7SsdxD1W-gfKJtUlEbvZ3i-4")
```

```
param h

a name=value string
```
Represents the "hash" element of a RecordEntry.

classes.name: installer.records.RecordEntry

methods.name: installer.records.RecordEntry.__init__
documentation:
Construct a `RecordEntry` object.

Most consumers should use {py:meth}`RecordEntry.from_elements`, since no
validation or parsing is performed by this constructor.

```
param path

file's path

param hash_

hash of the file's contents

param size

file's size in bytes
```

methods.name: installer.records.RecordEntry.to_row
documentation:
Convert this into a 3-element tuple that can be written in a RECORD file.

:param path_prefix: A prefix to attach to the path -- must end in `/`
:return: a (path, hash, size) row

methods.name: installer.records.RecordEntry.validate
documentation:
Validate that `data` matches this instance.

```
param data

Contents of the file corresponding to this instance.

return

whether data matches hash and size.
```

methods.name: installer.records.RecordEntry.from_elements
documentation:
Build a RecordEntry object, from values of the elements.

Typical usage:

```
for row in parse_record_file(f):
    record = RecordEntry.from_elements(row[0], row[1], row[2])
```

Meaning of each element is specified in {pep}`376`.

```
param path

first element (file's path)

param hash_

second element (hash of the file's contents)

param size

third element (file's size in bytes)

raises InvalidRecordEntry

if any element is invalid
```

Represents a single record in a RECORD file.

A list of {py:class}`RecordEntry` objects fully represents a RECORD file.

modules.name: installer.destinations
documentation: Handles all file writing and post-installation processing.

classes.name: installer.destinations.WheelDestination

methods.name: installer.destinations.WheelDestination.write_script
documentation:
Write a script in the correct location to invoke given entry point.

```
param name

name of the script

param module

module path, to load the entry point from

param attr

final attribute access, for the entry point

param section

Denotes the "entry point section" where this was specified.
Valid values are "gui" and "console".

type section

str
```

Example usage/behaviour:

```
>>> dest.write_script("pip", "pip._internal.cli", "main", "console")
```

methods.name: installer.destinations.WheelDestination.write_file
documentation:
Write a file to correct `path` within the `scheme`.

```
param scheme

scheme to write the file in (like "purelib", "platlib" etc).

param path

path within that scheme

param stream

contents of the file

param is_executable

whether the file should be made executable
```

The stream would be closed by the caller, after this call.

Example usage/behaviour:

```
>>> with open("__init__.py") as stream:
...     dest.write_file("purelib", "pkg/__init__.py", stream)
```

methods.name: installer.destinations.WheelDestination.finalize_installation
documentation:
Finalize installation, after all the files are written.

Handles (re)writing of the `RECORD` file.

```
param scheme

scheme to write the RECORD file in

param record_file_path

path of the RECORD file with that scheme

param records

entries to write to the RECORD file
```

Example usage/behaviour:

```
>>> dest.finalize_installation("purelib")
```

Handles writing the unpacked files, script generation and `RECORD` generation.

Subclasses provide the concrete script generation logic, as well as the RECORD file
(re)writing.

classes.name: installer.destinations.SchemeDictionaryDestination

methods.name: installer.destinations.SchemeDictionaryDestination.__init__
documentation:
Construct a `SchemeDictionaryDestination` object.

```
param scheme_dict

a mapping of {scheme: file-system-path}

param interpreter

the interpreter to use for generating scripts

param script_kind

the "kind" of launcher script to use

param hash_algorithm

the hashing algorithm to use, which is a member
of  (ideally from
).

param bytecode_optimization_levels

Compile cached bytecode for
installed .py files with these optimization levels. The bytecode
is specific to the minor version of Python (e.g. 3.10) used to
generate it.

param destdir

A staging directory in which to write all files. This
is expected to be the filesystem root at runtime, so embedded paths
will be written as though this was the root.
```

methods.name: installer.destinations.SchemeDictionaryDestination.write_to_fs
documentation:
Write contents of `stream` to the correct location on the filesystem.

```
param scheme

scheme to write the file in (like "purelib", "platlib" etc).

param path

path within that scheme

param stream

contents of the file

param is_executable

whether the file should be made executable
```

- Ensures that an existing file is not being overwritten.
- Hashes the written content, to determine the entry in the `RECORD` file.

methods.name: installer.destinations.SchemeDictionaryDestination.write_file
documentation:
Write a file to correct `path` within the `scheme`.

```
param scheme

scheme to write the file in (like "purelib", "platlib" etc).

param path

path within that scheme

param stream

contents of the file

param is_executable

whether the file should be made executable
```

- Changes the shebang for files in the "scripts" scheme.
- Uses {py:meth}`SchemeDictionaryDestination.write_to_fs` for the
  filesystem interaction.

methods.name: installer.destinations.SchemeDictionaryDestination.write_script
documentation:
Write a script to invoke an entrypoint.

```
param name

name of the script

param module

module path, to load the entry point from

param attr

final attribute access, for the entry point

param section

Denotes the "entry point section" where this was specified.
Valid values are "gui" and "console".

type section

str
```

- Generates a launcher using {any}`Script.generate`.
- Writes to the "scripts" scheme.
- Uses {py:meth}`SchemeDictionaryDestination.write_to_fs` for the
  filesystem interaction.

methods.name: installer.destinations.SchemeDictionaryDestination._compile_bytecode
documentation: Compile bytecode for a single .py file.

methods.name: installer.destinations.SchemeDictionaryDestination.finalize_installation
documentation:
Finalize installation, by writing the `RECORD` file & compiling bytecode.

```
param scheme

scheme to write the RECORD file in

param record_file_path

path of the RECORD file with that scheme

param records

entries to write to the RECORD file
```
Destination, based on a mapping of {scheme: file-system-path}.
